Plaintiff Federated Mutual Insurance Company'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF FEDERATED MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Semha Alwaya and Seth Wiener of the Law Offices of Semha Alwaya (510.595.7900) for plaintiff and moving party;
Kathleen E. Hegen of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (415.438.6656) for defendant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion is denied. Even assuming that Plaintiff could show some right to reimbursement, the amount of any reimbursement requires the Court to choose among at least six methodologies to determine the most equitable allocation based on all the facts and circumstances of the case. That task requires the Court to balance and weigh many competing factors based on the evidence that will be presented at trial and, accordingly, is not an appropriate determination on summary judgment. In any event, the Court sustains the objection to Exhibit N, so the date of tender remains an issue for trial. Plaintiff's separate statement does not separately set forth facts for any specific cause of action, and, therefore, does not allow for summary adjudication under Rule of Court 3.1350(b) and (e). Order signed. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (504)
